message:
Organize imports in lib.rs for consistency

Reorder import statements to follow Rust style conventions with local
module imports before standard library imports.

Import ordering change:
Move indexer::IndexOptions import before std::path::PathBuf to group
project-local imports ahead of standard library imports:

Before:
```rust
use clap::{Parser, Subcommand};
use std::path::PathBuf;
use indexer::IndexOptions;
```

After:
```rust
use clap::{Parser, Subcommand};
use indexer::IndexOptions;
use std::path::PathBuf;
```

Rust import ordering convention:
Standard practice (followed by rustfmt and Rust style guide):
1. External crate imports (clap, anyhow, tokio)
2. Local crate imports (crate::*, indexer::*, connectors::*)
3. Standard library imports (std::*)

Within each group, sort alphabetically (clap before connectors, indexer
before model, std::path before std::time).

Rationale:
- Consistency: Matches pattern in other modules (connectors/mod.rs,
  indexer/mod.rs already follow local-then-std ordering)
- Scannability: Developers look for project imports first when tracing
  dependencies, grouping them at top reduces visual search time
- Diff stability: When adding new imports, they slot into appropriate
  group without reordering unrelated imports (e.g., adding use model::
  types::Agent doesn't touch std::path::PathBuf line)

This is a mechanical formatting change with no functional impact—import
resolution is path-based, not order-based. Committed separately to keep
feature commits focused on logic changes, formatting commits focused on
style consistency.

Related: Other module imports (already following this convention),
rustfmt configuration (could enforce this automatically via group_imports
= "StdExternalCrate" in rustfmt.toml if we add it).
